task_completion.description: Import the pipeline PI points from the specified XYZ file into TopoCal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal interface showing an import dialog referencing 'C:\workspace\data\pipeline_pis.xyz' or the points successfully loaded into the main workspace.

task_completion.description: Create a continuous polyline connecting points 1, 2, 3, and 4 in order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A visible continuous line connecting four distinct points in the TopoCal graphical workspace.

task_completion.description: Generate new points along the polyline at 25-meter intervals.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A TopoCal tool/dialog box for point generation showing an interval setting of '25', or the workspace showing numerous (at least 20) evenly spaced new points along the drawn polyline.

task_completion.description: Export the generated points to stakeout_points.csv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog box in TopoCal or a file explorer window showing the creation/saving of 'C:\workspace\data\stakeout_points.csv'.

task_completion.description: Save the TopoCal project to pipeline_project.top.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog box in TopoCal saving to 'C:\workspace\data\pipeline_project.top', or the application title bar updating to reflect this filename.

integrity.description: The agent used the TopoCal graphical interface to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with TopoCal menus, dialogs, and the graphical workspace rather than using terminal commands or a text editor to mock the files.

integrity.description: The exported CSV and saved project were generated via TopoCal's built-in export/save functions.
integrity.visual_evidence: Save/Export dialog boxes native to TopoCal are visible, rather than the agent writing a python script or using echo/Out-File commands in PowerShell to create dummy files.

integrity.description: The agent actually generated the points along the polyline using TopoCal's tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The TopoCal workspace visually displays the newly generated points along the polyline before exporting, rather than just exporting an empty or manually typed dataset.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Input file: C:\workspace\data\pipeline_pis.xyz. Expected export CSV: C:\workspace\data\stakeout_points.csv. Expected project save path: C:\workspace\data\pipeline_project.top. The interval for generated points must be exactly 25.0 meters, which should result in a minimum of 20 generated points along the polyline.
